/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — general-affine-open Serre vanishing (Need #2)

The declarations below **enlarge** the affine cover system from the distinguished-open basis
`{D f}` to the basis of *all* affine opens of `Spec R`, so that the basis-comparison criterion
`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` yields `Ext^q(jShriekOU V, H) = 0` for any affine open `V` (not just a
distinguished `D f`), entirely inside `(Spec R).Modules` (no restriction functor). The route is the
ambient `enlarge-B` of `analogies/change-of-scheme-cohomology.md`: the only structurally new pieces
are the generalisations of `standard_cover_cofinal` and `affine_surj_of_vanishing` from `D f` to a
general affine open (swapping `PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen` for `IsAffineOpen.isCompact`); the
`faces_mem`/`injective_acyclic` fields are unchanged (faces are distinguished ⊆ affine; injective
acyclicity is cover-agnostic). The positive-degree quasi-coherent Čech-vanishing *seed* over covers
of a general affine open is the single remaining geometric residual; it is carried as an explicit
hypothesis `hseed` (the general analogue of `affine_tildeVanishing`, needing a change-of-base to
`Γ(V)` rather than to a single `R_f`). -/

/-- **Distinguished opens of `Spec R` are affine opens.**  `D(r) ⊆ Spec R` is affine, being
isomorphic to `Spec R[1/r]` via `basicOpenIsoSpecAway`.  Project-local: places each distinguished
open into the enlarged affine-open basis, discharging `faces_mem` for `affineCoverSystemGeneral`. -/
theorem isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (r : R) :
    IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r) :=
  IsAffine.of_isIso (basicOpenIsoSpecAway r).hom

/-- **Standard covers are cofinal among open covers of a general affine open** (Stacks 02KG,
Tag 009L), generalising `standard_cover_cofinal` from a distinguished `D(f)` to an *arbitrary affine
open* `V` of `Spec R`.  Given an arbitrary open cover `W : α → (Spec R).Opens` of `V`, there is a
finite standard subcover refining it: finitely many `g : Fin n → R` with `V = ⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` and each
`D(gᵢ) ≤ W (φ i)`.  The only change from `standard_cover_cofinal` is the source of quasi-compactness:
`IsAffineOpen.isCompact` in place of `PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen`.  Project-local: the
refinement step for `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine` over a general affine open. -/
theorem standard_cover_cofinal_affine {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (V : (Spec R).Opens)
    (hV : IsAffineOpen V) {α : Type u} (W : α → (Spec R).Opens) (hcov : V ≤ ⨆ a, W a) :
    ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → α),
      V = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ∧
      ∀ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ W (φ i) := by
  classical
  have hK : IsCompact (V : Set ↥(Spec R)) := hV.isCompact
  have hbasis := PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens (R := R)
  -- `B r` is the distinguished open `D(r)` typed as an open of `Spec R`.
  let B : R → (Spec R).Opens := fun r => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
  -- Index type: basic opens contained in `V ⊓ W a`, carrying the witness `a`.
  let I := {p : R × α // B p.1 ≤ V ⊓ W p.2}
  let cover : I → Set ↥(Spec R) := fun p => (B p.1.1 : Set ↥(Spec R))
  have hopen : ∀ i, IsOpen (cover i) := fun i => (B i.1.1).isOpen
  -- The chosen basic opens cover `V`.
  have hsub : (V : Set ↥(Spec R)) ⊆ ⋃ i, cover i := by
    intro x hx
    have hxV : x ∈ V := hx
    obtain ⟨a, hxa⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp (hcov hxV)
    have hxinf : x ∈ ((V ⊓ W a : (Spec R).Opens) : Set ↥(Spec R)) := by
      rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inf]
      exact ⟨hxV, hxa⟩
    obtain ⟨v, hvrange, hxv, hvsub⟩ :=
      hbasis.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxinf (V ⊓ W a).isOpen
    obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := hvrange
    have hr' : (B r : Set ↥(Spec R)) = v := hr
    have hle : B r ≤ V ⊓ W a := by
      rw [← SetLike.coe_subset_coe, hr']; exact hvsub
    refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨⟨(r, a), hle⟩, ?_⟩
    change x ∈ (B r : Set ↥(Spec R))
    rw [hr']; exact hxv
  -- Quasi-compactness: extract a finite subcover.
  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := hK.elim_finite_subcover cover hopen hsub
  -- Repackage the finite index set `t : Finset I` as `Fin n`.
  let e := t.equivFin
  refine ⟨t.card, fun i => (e.symm i).1.1.1, fun i => (e.symm i).1.1.2, ?_, ?_⟩
  · apply le_antisymm
    · intro x hx
      obtain ⟨i, hit, hxi⟩ := Set.mem_iUnion₂.mp (ht hx)
      rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
      refine ⟨e ⟨i, hit⟩, ?_⟩
      change x ∈ B (e.symm (e ⟨i, hit⟩)).1.1.1
      rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]; exact hxi
    · rw [iSup_le_iff]
      intro i
      exact le_trans (e.symm i).1.2 inf_le_left
  · intro i
    exact le_trans (e.symm i).1.2 inf_le_right

end AlgebraicGeometry

`IsAffineOpen.isCompact` in place of `PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen`.  Project-local: the
refinement step for `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine` over a general affine open. -/
theorem standard_cover_cofinal_affine {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (V : (Spec R).Opens)
    (hV : IsAffineOpen V) {α : Type u} (W : α → (Spec R).Opens) (hcov : V ≤ ⨆ a, W a) :
    ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R) (φ : Fin n → α),
      V = ⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) ∧
      ∀ i, (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i) : (Spec R).Opens) ≤ W (φ i) := by
  classical
  have hK : IsCompact (V : Set ↥(Spec R)) := hV.isCompact
  have hbasis := PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens (R := R)
  -- `B r` is the distinguished open `D(r)` typed as an open of `Spec R`.
  let B : R → (Spec R).Opens := fun r => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r
  -- Index type: basic opens contained in `V ⊓ W a`, carrying the witness `a`.
  let I := {p : R × α // B p.1 ≤ V ⊓ W p.2}
  let cover : I → Set ↥(Spec R) := fun p => (B p.1.1 : Set ↥(Spec R))
  have hopen : ∀ i, IsOpen (cover i) := fun i => (B i.1.1).isOpen
  -- The chosen basic opens cover `V`.
  have hsub : (V : Set ↥(Spec R)) ⊆ ⋃ i, cover i := by
    intro x hx
    have hxV : x ∈ V := hx
    obtain ⟨a, hxa⟩ := TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mp (hcov hxV)
    have hxinf : x ∈ ((V ⊓ W a : (Spec R).Opens) : Set ↥(Spec R)) := by
      rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_inf]
      exact ⟨hxV, hxa⟩
    obtain ⟨v, hvrange, hxv, hvsub⟩ :=
      hbasis.exists_subset_of_mem_open hxinf (V ⊓ W a).isOpen
    obtain ⟨r, hr⟩ := hvrange
    have hr' : (B r : Set ↥(Spec R)) = v := hr
    have hle : B r ≤ V ⊓ W a := by
      rw [← SetLike.coe_subset_coe, hr']; exact hvsub
    refine Set.mem_iUnion.mpr ⟨⟨(r, a), hle⟩, ?_⟩
    change x ∈ (B r : Set ↥(Spec R))
    rw [hr']; exact hxv
  -- Quasi-compactness: extract a finite subcover.
  obtain ⟨t, ht⟩ := hK.elim_finite_subcover cover hopen hsub
  -- Repackage the finite index set `t : Finset I` as `Fin n`.
  let e := t.equivFin
  refine ⟨t.card, fun i => (e.symm i).1.1.1, fun i => (e.symm i).1.1.2, ?_, ?_⟩
  · apply le_antisymm
    · intro x hx
      obtain ⟨i, hit, hxi⟩ := Set.mem_iUnion₂.mp (ht hx)
      rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]
      refine ⟨e ⟨i, hit⟩, ?_⟩
      change x ∈ B (e.symm (e ⟨i, hit⟩)).1.1.1
      rw [Equiv.symm_apply_apply]; exact hxi
    · rw [iSup_le_iff]
      intro i
      exact le_trans (e.symm i).1.2 inf_le_left
  · intro i
    exact le_trans (e.symm i).1.2 inf_le_right

end AlgebraicGeometry

set_option maxHeartbeats 1600000 in
-- The `ses_cech_h1` assembly over a general affine open is heartbeat-heavy (large product
-- presheaf-section equalities); the bound matches `affine_surj_of_vanishing`.
/-- **Section surjectivity for the enlarged affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG, `surj_of_vanishing`
field, general-affine-open form), generalising `affine_surj_of_vanishing` from a distinguished `D f`
to an *arbitrary affine open* `V₀`.  Let `S : 0 → S₁ → S₂ → S₃ → 0` be short exact with `S₁` having
vanishing positive Čech cohomology over every standard cover `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` *whose union is affine*
(`hvanish`). Then the section map `S₂(V₀) → S₃(V₀)` is surjective over every affine open `V₀`.

Same proof as `affine_surj_of_vanishing`, with the quasi-compact refinement supplied by
`standard_cover_cofinal_affine` (using `IsAffineOpen.isCompact`) and the cover's affineness
(`V₀ = ⨆ D(gᵢ)`) fed to `hvanish`. Project-local: discharges the `surj_of_vanishing` field of
`affineCoverSystemGeneral`. -/
theorem affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (S : ShortComplex (Spec R).Modules) (hS : S.ShortExact)
    (hvanish : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
      IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
          (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) →
      ∀ (q : ℕ), 0 < q →
        IsZero (cechCohomology (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
          ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₁) q))
    (V₀ : (Spec R).Opens) (hV₀ : IsAffineOpen V₀) :
    Function.Surjective (ConcreteCategory.hom
      (((PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
        ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g)).app
          (Opposite.op V₀))) := by
  classical
  set FX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₁ with hFX
  set GX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₂ with hGX
  set HX := (Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj S.X₃ with hHX
  set fι := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.f) with hfι
  set gπ := (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf (Spec R).ringCatSheaf.obj).map
    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).map S.g) with hgπ
  -- Step 1: a module epi becomes a locally surjective map of abelian presheaves.
  haveI hgepi : Epi S.g := hS.epi_g
  have hepiTS : Epi ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g) :=
    @Functor.map_epi _ _ _ _ (SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf)
      (toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf) _ _ S.g hgepi
  have hls : TopCat.Presheaf.IsLocallySurjective gπ :=
    (Sheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff_epi' AddCommGrpCat.{u}
      ((SheafOfModules.toSheaf.{u} (Spec R).ringCatSheaf).map S.g)).mpr hepiTS
  rw [TopCat.Presheaf.isLocallySurjective_iff] at hls
  intro t
  -- Step 2: per-point local lifts of `t`.
  have hch : ∀ p : ↥V₀, ∃ (W : (Spec R).Opens) (hWle : W ≤ V₀)
      (sl : ToType (GX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op W))),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (Opposite.op W)) sl
        = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE hWle).op) t ∧ (p : Spec R) ∈ W := by
    intro p
    obtain ⟨W, hWle, ⟨sl, hsl⟩, hmem⟩ := hls V₀ t p.1 p.2
    exact ⟨W, hWle, sl, hsl, hmem⟩
  choose W hWle sLift hsLift hmem using hch
  have hVcov : V₀ ≤ ⨆ p : ↥V₀, W p := by
    intro x hx
    exact TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨⟨x, hx⟩, hmem ⟨x, hx⟩⟩
  -- Step 3: refine to a standard cover `D(gᵢ)` of the affine open `V₀`.
  obtain ⟨n, g, φ, hVeq, hgle⟩ := standard_cover_cofinal_affine V₀ hV₀ W hVcov
  set U : ULift.{u} (Fin n) → (Spec R).Opens := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down) with hU
  have hUsup : ⨆ i, U i = V₀ := by
    rw [hU, hVeq]
    exact Equiv.ulift.{u}.iSup_comp (g := fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i))
  -- the cover's union is affine (it equals `V₀`), so it is an admissible covering.
  have haff : IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
      (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) := by
    have heq : (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) = V₀ := hUsup
    rw [heq]; exact hV₀
  -- the restricted local lifts on the standard cover
  set sLoc : ∀ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), ToType (GX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (U i))) :=
    fun i => ConcreteCategory.hom (GX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op)
      (sLift (φ i.down)) with hsLoc
  -- the section `t` transported to `⨆ U` (`= V₀`)
  have hopV : (Opposite.op (⨆ i, U i) : (TopologicalSpace.Opens (Spec R))ᵒᵖ) = Opposite.op V₀ :=
    congrArg Opposite.op hUsup
  set s : ToType (HX.presheaf.obj (Opposite.op (⨆ i, U i))) :=
    ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)) t with hs
  -- assemble the hypotheses of `ses_cech_h1`
  have hπι : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (FX.presheaf.obj V)),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) x) = 0 := by
    intro V x
    have hz : fι ≫ gπ = 0 := by
      rw [hfι, hgπ, ← Functor.map_comp, ← Functor.map_comp, S.zero,
        Functor.map_zero, Functor.map_zero]
    have := congrArg (fun (ψ : FX.presheaf ⟶ HX.presheaf) => ConcreteCategory.hom (ψ.app V) x) hz
    simpa using this
  have hmono : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ),
      Function.Injective (ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V)) := by
    intro V
    haveI hpzm : (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).PreservesZeroMorphisms := by
      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
    haveI hpfl : PreservesFiniteLimits (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) := by
      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
    haveI : Mono S.f := hS.mono_f
    have hmonoAb : Mono (fι.app V) :=
      inferInstanceAs (Mono ((sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).map S.f))
    rwa [AddCommGrpCat.mono_iff_injective] at hmonoAb
  have hker : ∀ (V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(Spec R))ᵒᵖ) (x : ToType (GX.presheaf.obj V)),
      ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app V) x = 0 → ∃ y, ConcreteCategory.hom (fι.app V) y = x := by
    intro V x hx
    haveI hpzm : (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)).PreservesZeroMorphisms := by
      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
    haveI hpfl : PreservesFiniteLimits (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) := by
      unfold sectionsFunctor; infer_instance
    have hex : (S.map (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V))).Exact :=
      ShortComplex.Exact.map_of_mono_of_preservesKernel hS.exact
        (sectionsFunctor (Opposite.unop V)) hS.mono_f inferInstance
    rw [ShortComplex.ab_exact_iff_function_exact] at hex
    exact (hex x).mp hx
  -- the vanishing input, packaged as homology of the section Čech complex
  have hH1 : IsZero ((sectionCechComplex U FX).homology 1) := hvanish n g haff 1 one_pos
  -- the local-lift compatibility `gπ(sLocᵢ) = s |_ Uᵢ`
  have hlift : ∀ i, ConcreteCategory.hom (gπ.app (Opposite.op (U i))) (sLoc i)
      = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (TopologicalSpace.Opens.leSupr U i).op) s := by
    intro i
    simp only [hsLoc, hs]
    erw [gπ.naturality_apply (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op (sLift (φ i.down)), hsLift (φ i.down)]
    show ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op)
        (ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hWle (φ i.down))).op) t)
      = ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (TopologicalSpace.Opens.leSupr U i).op)
        (ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)) t)
    have hmaps : HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hWle (φ i.down))).op
          ≫ HX.presheaf.map (homOfLE (hgle i.down)).op
        = HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)
          ≫ HX.presheaf.map (TopologicalSpace.Opens.leSupr U i).op := by
      rw [← HX.presheaf.map_comp, ← HX.presheaf.map_comp]
      congr 1
    simp only [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply]
    rw [← hmaps]
    exact (ConcreteCategory.comp_apply _ _ t).symm
  -- apply the Čech surjectivity criterion
  obtain ⟨glob, hglob⟩ := ses_cech_h1 U FX GX HX fι gπ S.X₂.isSheaf S.X₃.isSheaf
    hπι hmono hker s hH1 sLoc hlift
  -- transport the global lift back to `V₀`
  refine ⟨ConcreteCategory.hom (GX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV)) glob, ?_⟩
  erw [gπ.naturality_apply (eqToHom hopV) glob, hglob]
  show ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV))
      (ConcreteCategory.hom (HX.presheaf.map (eqToHom hopV.symm)) t) = t
  rw [← ConcreteCategory.comp_apply, ← HX.presheaf.map_comp]
  simp only [eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_refl, CategoryTheory.Functor.map_id]
  rfl

end AlgebraicGeometry

set_option maxHeartbeats 2000000 in
-- The structure literal forces three nontrivial field elaborations (`faces_mem`,
-- `surj_of_vanishing`, `injective_acyclic`), each unfolding large presheaf data.
/-- **The enlarged affine cover system** (Stacks 02KG), with basis `B` the *all affine opens* of
`Spec R` (rather than only the distinguished `D f` of `affineCoverSystem`) and admissible coverings
`Cov` the standard finite covers `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` whose union `⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` is affine.  The three fields:
- `faces_mem` ← faces `⨅ₖ D(g_{σk}) = D(∏ g)` are distinguished, hence affine
  (`isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen`);
- `surj_of_vanishing` ← `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine` (general-affine-open section surjectivity);
- `injective_acyclic` ← `injective_cech_acyclicFam` (cover-agnostic injective Čech-acyclicity).
Project-local: the enlarged instantiation of `BasisCovSystem` consumed by
`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` to obtain general-affine-open Serre vanishing. -/
noncomputable def affineCoverSystemGeneral (R : CommRingCat.{u}) : BasisCovSystem (Spec R) where
  B := { U : (Spec R).Opens | IsAffineOpen U }
  Cov := { c : CovDatum (Spec R) | ∃ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
    c = ⟨ULift.{u} (Fin n), fun i => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)⟩ ∧
    IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
      (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) }
  faces_mem := by
    rintro c ⟨n, g, rfl, hcov⟩ p σ
    have h := basicOpen_sprod (p + 1) (fun j : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => g j.down) σ
    exact h ▸ isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen
      (∏ k, (fun j : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => g j.down) (σ k))
  surj_of_vanishing := by

/-! ## Project-local Mathlib supplement — general-affine-open Serre vanishing (reduced to the seed)

The **residual** of the enlarged route is the positive-degree quasi-coherent Čech-vanishing *seed*
`HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F`: vanishing of `Ȟᵖ(𝒰, F)` for `p > 0` over
every standard cover `𝒰 : i ↦ D(gᵢ)` *whose union `⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` is affine* (not just a distinguished
`D(f)`). Unlike the distinguished case — discharged by `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway`
via change of base to a single `Rf = Localization.Away f` — a general affine open `V = ⨆ᵢ D(gᵢ)` has
no single `f` with `V = D(f)`, so the seed needs a change of base to `Γ(V, 𝒪)` (equivalently, the
identification of the section Čech complex over `{D(gᵢ) ⊆ Spec R}` with the standard-cover complex of
`~(Γ(V,F))` over `Spec Γ(V)` under `V ≅ Spec Γ(V)`). That change-of-scheme identification is the lone
remaining geometric leaf; it is isolated here as the explicit hypothesis `hseed`. -/

/-- **Serre vanishing for a general affine open, reduced to the Čech-vanishing seed** (Stacks 02KG,
generalised from `D(f)` to an arbitrary affine open).  For a quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`
and **any** affine open `V` of `Spec R`, the absolute cohomology `Hᵖ(V, F) = Extᵖ(jShriekOU V, F)`
vanishes for `p > 0`, modulo the general-affine-open Čech-vanishing seed `hseed`.  Instantiates the
basis-comparison criterion `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` at the enlarged cover system
`affineCoverSystemGeneral R` (whose basis is all affine opens).  Project-local: the Need #2 target of
`analogies/change-of-scheme-cohomology.md`, isolating the single seed residual. -/
theorem affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
    (hseed : HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F)
    (V : (Spec R).Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) F p) : e = 0 :=
  cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) hseed V hV p hp e

end AlgebraicGeometry

generalised from `D(f)` to an arbitrary affine open).  For a quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`
and **any** affine open `V` of `Spec R`, the absolute cohomology `Hᵖ(V, F) = Extᵖ(jShriekOU V, F)`
vanishes for `p > 0`, modulo the general-affine-open Čech-vanishing seed `hseed`.  Instantiates the

`affineCoverSystemGeneral R` (whose basis is all affine opens).  Project-local: the Need #2 target of
`analogies/change-of-scheme-cohomology.md`, isolating the single seed residual. -/
theorem affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
    (hseed : HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F)
    (V : (Spec R).Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) F p) : e = 0 :=

/-- **Reduction of the general-affine-open quasi-coherent seed to the tilde case.**  For a
quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`, transporting along `F ≅ ~M` (`qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`,
`M = Γ(Spec R, F)`) reduces the seed `HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F` to the
tilde-case positive-degree Čech vanishing `htilde`: vanishing of `Ȟᵖ(𝒰, ~M)` over every standard
cover `i ↦ D(gᵢ)` whose union is affine.  Verbatim analogue of
`affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing` for the enlarged cover system; isolates the single
geometric residual (the change-of-base-to-`Γ(V)` tilde-section identification).  Project-local: the
cleanest statement of the general-affine-open seed residual. -/
theorem affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
    (htilde : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
        IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
            (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) →
        ∀ p, 0 < p →
          IsZero (cechCohomology
            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
              (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p)) :
    HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F := by
  intro c hc p hp
  obtain ⟨n, g, rfl, hcov⟩ := hc
  refine cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso _
    ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).mapIso
      (qcoh_iso_tilde_sections F).symm) p ?_
  exact htilde n g hcov p hp

/-- **Serre vanishing for a general affine open, reduced to the Čech-vanishing seed** (Stacks 02KG,
generalised from `D(f)` to an arbitrary affine open).  For a quasi-coherent `𝒪_{Spec R}`-module `F`
and **any** affine open `V` of `Spec R`, the absolute cohomology `Hᵖ(V, F) = Extᵖ(jShriekOU V, F)`
vanishes for `p > 0`, modulo the general-affine-open Čech-vanishing seed `hseed`.  Instantiates the
basis-comparison criterion `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` at the enlarged cover system
`affineCoverSystemGeneral R` (whose basis is all affine opens).  Project-local: the Need #2 target of
`analogies/change-of-scheme-cohomology.md`, isolating the single seed residual. -/
theorem affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
    (hseed : HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F)
    (V : (Spec R).Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) F p) : e = 0 :=
  cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) hseed V hV p hp e

/-- **Serre vanishing for a general affine open, reduced to the tilde Čech-vanishing leaf**
(Stacks 02KG, generalised).  Composes `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing` (seed
from tilde vanishing) with `affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed`: for quasi-coherent `F` and any
affine open `V`, `Extᵖ(jShriekOU V, F) = 0` for `p > 0`, modulo the tilde-section change-of-base leaf
`htilde`.  Project-local: the fully-reduced Need #2 top, leaving exactly the change-of-scheme tilde
residual. -/
theorem affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing {R : CommRingCat.{u}}
    [EnoughInjectives (Spec R).Modules] (F : (Spec R).Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
    (htilde : ∀ (n : ℕ) (g : Fin n → R),
        IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R)
            (⨆ i : ULift.{u} (Fin n), PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down)) →
        ∀ p, 0 < p →
          IsZero (cechCohomology
            (fun i : ULift.{u} (Fin n) => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i.down))
            ((Scheme.Modules.toPresheafOfModules (Spec R)).obj
              (tilde (moduleSpecΓFunctor.obj F))) p))
    (V : (Spec R).Opens) (hV : IsAffineOpen V) (p : ℕ) (hp : 0 < p)
    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext (jShriekOU V) F p) : e = 0 :=
  affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed F
    (affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing F htilde) V hV p hp e

end AlgebraicGeometry

input.content:
# AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/AffineSerreVanishing.lean

## Summary
- **Declarations added (7, all axiom-clean):**
  - `isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen` (line ~545)
  - `standard_cover_cofinal_affine` (line ~561)
  - `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine` (line ~620)
  - `affineCoverSystemGeneral` (line ~745, the enlarged `BasisCovSystem`)
  - `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing` (line ~795)
  - `affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed` (line ~818)
  - `affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing` (line ~834)
- **Declarations blocked (1):** the general-affine-open Čech-vanishing **seed**
  `HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F` for quasi-coherent `F` — needs a
  change-of-base to `Γ(V,𝒪)` (genuinely a change-of-scheme of the *section* Čech complex, NOT the
  injectives wall). Isolated as the explicit hypothesis `hseed` / `htilde`.
- **Sorry count in file: 0 → 0** (no sorries introduced; the residual is a hypothesis, not a sorry).
- Kernel build `lake env lean AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/AffineSerreVanishing.lean` → exit 0.
- `#print axioms` on all 7 new decls → `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` only.

## What was built (Need #2 — enlarge `B` from `{D f}` to all affine opens)

The analogist route in `analogies/change-of-scheme-cohomology.md` was followed exactly: enlarge the
affine cover system's basis to all affine opens, keep `Cov` = standard covers, read off
general-affine-open `Ext`-vanishing from `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` (no restriction functor, fully
ambient). All three `BasisCovSystem` fields are discharged:
- `faces_mem` ← faces `⨅ₖ D(g_{σk}) = D(∏ g)` are distinguished, hence affine
  (`isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen`, via `basicOpenIsoSpecAway` + `IsAffine.of_isIso`).
- `surj_of_vanishing` ← `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine` (the one structurally new piece;
  verbatim generalisation of `affine_surj_of_vanishing` with `IsAffineOpen.isCompact` swapped in via
  `standard_cover_cofinal_affine`, and the cover's affineness `⨆ D(gᵢ) = V₀` fed to `hvanish`).
- `injective_acyclic` ← `injective_cech_acyclicFam` (cover-agnostic, unchanged).

The qcoh seed is reduced to its tilde form by
`affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing` (verbatim analogue of the existing
`affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_of_tildeVanishing`, transporting along `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`), and the
two tops `affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed` / `_of_tildeVanishing` instantiate
`cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` at the enlarged system.

## isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen — RESOLVED
- **Approach:** `IsAffine.of_isIso (basicOpenIsoSpecAway r).hom` (`D(r) ≅ Spec R[1/r]`, affine).
- **Dead end / gotcha:** a *bare statement* `IsAffineOpen (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens)`
  FAILS to elaborate (the ascription is not respected — `Opens (PrimeSpectrum R)` vs `Scheme.Opens ?m`
  mismatch). FIX: give `X` explicitly — `IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)` — or
  route through the `(Spec R).Opens`-typed `specBasicOpen`. Used the `(X := Spec R)` form throughout.

## standard_cover_cofinal_affine — RESOLVED
- **Approach:** verbatim copy of `standard_cover_cofinal`, with `Uf := D(f)` replaced by the general
  affine open `V` and `PrimeSpectrum.isCompact_basicOpen f` replaced by `hV.isCompact`.
- **Dead end / gotcha:** `rw [TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup]` and `rw [iSup_le_iff]` FAIL on the
  general goal (the iSup's underlying type elaborates as `Opens (PrimeSpectrum R)` while membership is
  `Opens ↥(Spec R)` — defeq but not syntactic, so `rw` can't match). FIX: use term-mode
  `TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨…, …⟩` and `iSup_le fun i => …` (these unify up to defeq). The
  same pitfall recurs for the `IsAffineOpen (⨆ …) ` goal — discharged by `hUsup ▸ hV₀` (term `▸`, not
  `rw`).

## affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine — RESOLVED
- **Approach:** verbatim copy of `affine_surj_of_vanishing` with: `(f : R)` → `(V₀, hV₀ : IsAffineOpen
  V₀)`; the `hvanish` covering-witness `(f' : R)(D(f')=⨆…)` → `IsAffineOpen (⨆ …)`;
  `standard_cover_cofinal` → `standard_cover_cofinal_affine`; the cover's affineness produced by
  `hUsup ▸ hV₀`. Everything downstream (`ses_cech_h1`, the section transport) is unchanged.
- `maxHeartbeats 1600000` (matches the original).

## affineCoverSystemGeneral — RESOLVED
- `B := { U : (Spec R).Opens | IsAffineOpen U }`,
  `Cov := { c | ∃ n g, c = ⟨ULift (Fin n), i ↦ D(gᵢ.down)⟩ ∧ IsAffineOpen (⨆ i, D(gᵢ.down)) }`.
- The affineness of the union is the covering condition (mirrors the memory rule: an over-broad `Cov`
  makes the qcoh seed false; `{D x, D y}` on `k[x,y]` is excluded because its union is non-affine).
- **Gotcha:** the `injective_acyclic` field timed out at `maxHeartbeats 2000000` when written as a bare
  `exact injective_cech_acyclicFam …`; FIX (matching the existing `affineCoverSystem`) = bind it first:
  `have hfam := injective_cech_acyclicFam …; exact hfam`.

## The seed (NOT added — the lone residual)
- **Statement needed:** for `M : ModuleCat R`, a family `g : Fin n → R` with
  `IsAffineOpen (⨆ i : ULift (Fin n), D(gᵢ.down))`, and `p > 0`:
  `IsZero (cechCohomology (fun i ↦ D(gᵢ.down)) ((toPresheafOfModules).obj (tilde M)) p)`.
  (This is exactly the `htilde` hypothesis of `affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing`.)
- **Why the existing tool does not apply:** `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway`
  (`CechAcyclic.lean:1868`) requires `D(f) = ⨆ D(gᵢ)` for a *single* `f` and changes base to
  `Rf = Localization.Away f`. A general affine open `V = ⨆ D(gᵢ)` is **not** `D(f)` for any `f` (such
  affine opens exist), so there is no single-element localisation to use. The section Čech homology
  depends only on `{gᵢ}` and `M`, so the obstruction is intrinsic to the family, not removable by
  reindexing.
- **Sound route (NOT the injectives wall):** `V` is affine, `V ≅ Spec Γ(V)` (`IsAffineOpen.isoSpec` /
  `Scheme.isoSpec`). The `D(gᵢ) ⊆ V` become a standard cover of `Spec Γ(V)` by basic opens whose
  elements span the unit ideal of `S := Γ(V,𝒪)`. Identify the section Čech complex over
  `{D(gᵢ) ⊆ Spec R}` of `~M` with the standard-cover complex of `~(M ⊗_R S)` (= `~(Γ(V,~M))`) over
  `Spec S`, then apply the EXISTING `sectionCech_affine_vanishing` (full-span case) over `S`. This is a
  concrete **change-of-scheme of the section complex** (NOT `Ext`, NOT injectives — so it does NOT
  resurrect restriction-preserves-injectives). Estimated ~150–300 LOC, comparable to the
  `QcohTildeSections` section-localisation build. It was NOT attempted here because it cannot be left
  half-done (no-sorry invariant) and is a multi-iteration build on its own.
- **Approaches ruled out:** (1) keep `Cov` = covers of `D(f)` only — then `surj_of_vanishing` for a
  general affine `V ∈ B` cannot feed `hvanish` (the refining cover of `V` is not in `Cov`); the seed is
  unavoidable. (2) derive the general seed from the existing distinguished seed `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh`
  — impossible, the cover sets are disjoint in shape.

## Why I stopped
- **Real progress:** 7 axiom-clean declarations added (named above with line numbers), kernel-verified
  (`lake env lean` exit 0), all `#print axioms`-clean. This completes the Need #2 cover-system
  enlargement end-to-end **modulo the single seed leaf**, which is now isolated as the precise tilde
  hypothesis `htilde` of `affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing`.
- **Partial:** the general-affine-open seed is blocked on a genuine change-of-scheme build (precise
  statement + sound route above). The analogist's "~40–80 LOC, no new gap" estimate **missed** that
  `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` also consumes `HasVanishingHigherCech` (the seed), which for the
  enlarged `Cov` is general-affine-open Čech vanishing — a real change-of-scheme leaf, not mechanical.
- **Informal agent:** no usable LLM API key in env (`GEMINI_CLI_*` are IDE server vars, not an API key).

## Needs blueprint entry
All 7 new non-private declarations are `lean_aux` nodes with no blueprint block yet (they will appear in
`archon dag-query unmatched`). Blueprint chapter: `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`. Suggested
labels + dependencies:
- `isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen` — `D(r)` affine via `basicOpenIsoSpecAway`. Deps: `basicOpenIsoSpecAway`,
  `IsAffine.of_isIso`.
- `standard_cover_cofinal_affine` — Stacks 009L for a general affine open. Deps:
  `IsAffineOpen.isCompact`, `PrimeSpectrum.isTopologicalBasis_basic_opens`. Generalises
  `lem:standard_cover_cofinal`.
- `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine` — Stacks `lemma-ses-cech-h1`, general-affine-open form. Deps:
  `ses_cech_h1`, `toSheaf_preservesEpimorphisms`, `standard_cover_cofinal_affine`. Generalises the
  `surj_of_vanishing` blueprint env.
- `affineCoverSystemGeneral` — enlarged `def:affine_cover_system` (basis = all affine opens). Deps:
  `affine_faces_mem`/`isAffineOpen_specBasicOpen`, `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine`,
  `injective_cech_acyclicFam`.
- `affine_cech_vanishing_qcoh_general_of_tildeVanishing` — general seed from the tilde leaf. Deps:
  `qcoh_iso_tilde_sections`, `cechCohomology_isZero_of_iso`.
- `affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed` / `affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_tildeVanishing` —
  `lem:affine_serre_vanishing_general_open` (the Need #2 TODO target). Deps:
  `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis`, `affineCoverSystemGeneral`, and (for `_of_tildeVanishing`) the
  reduction lemma. The remaining open leaf is the tilde seed `htilde` (change-of-base to `Γ(V)`).

---
name: need2-general-affine-cover-system
description: Need#2 general-affine-open Serre vanishing — cover-system enlargement DONE; lone residual is the change-of-scheme tilde seed (NOT the injectives wall)
metadata:
  type: project
---

iter-056 (AffineSerreVanishing.lean): built the **enlarged** affine cover system
`affineCoverSystemGeneral R` (`B = {all affine opens}`, `Cov = standard covers with affine union`) +
`standard_cover_cofinal_affine` + `affine_surj_of_vanishing_affine` + the two reduction tops
`affine_serre_vanishing_general_of_seed`/`_of_tildeVanishing` — all axiom-clean. This is the analogist
"enlarge-B" route of [[change-of-scheme-cohomology]] (Need#2). Gives `Ext^q(jShriekOU V, F)=0` for ANY
affine open V, qcoh F, modulo ONE residual.

**Residual (the lone open leaf):** the general-affine-open Čech-vanishing SEED
`HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F` = `htilde`: for `g : Fin n → R` with
`IsAffineOpen (⨆ i:ULift, D(gᵢ.down))`, `IsZero (cechCohomology {D(gᵢ.down)} (~M) p)` for p>0.
The analogist's "~40–80 LOC, no gap" estimate MISSED this — `cech_eq_cohomology_of_basis` also consumes
the seed, which for the enlarged Cov is general-affine Čech vanishing. `sectionCech_homology_exact_of_localizationAway`
does NOT apply (needs `D(f)=⨆D(gᵢ)`; general affine V is not any single `D(f)`). Sound route =
change-of-scheme of the SECTION complex along `V≅Spec Γ(V)` (`IsAffineOpen.isoSpec`), identify with the
full-span complex of `~(Γ(V,F))` over `Spec Γ(V)`, apply existing `sectionCech_affine_vanishing`. This is
concrete sections, NOT Ext/injectives — so it does NOT resurrect restriction-preserves-injectives. ~150–300
LOC, a separate multi-iter build.

**Two API traps caught (reusable):**
1. `IsAffineOpen (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r : (Spec R).Opens)` as a bare STATEMENT fails to elaborate
   (ascription not respected). FIX: `IsAffineOpen (X := Spec R) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen r)`. Proof:
   `IsAffine.of_isIso (basicOpenIsoSpecAway r).hom`.
2. Over `(Spec R).Opens`, `⨆ i, PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (g i)` has underlying type `Opens (PrimeSpectrum R)`
   but membership/≤ is at `Opens ↥(Spec R)` (defeq, NOT syntactic) — so `rw [Opens.mem_iSup]`,
   `rw [iSup_le_iff]`, `rw [hUsup]` all FAIL with "did not find pattern". FIX: term-mode
   `Opens.mem_iSup.mpr ⟨…⟩`, `iSup_le fun i => …`, `hUsup ▸ hV₀` (unify up to defeq).
3. `affineCoverSystemGeneral.injective_acyclic` field TIMES OUT at 2000000 hb as bare
   `exact injective_cech_acyclicFam …`; bind first: `have hfam := …; exact hfam` (matches existing
   `affineCoverSystem`).

Next: discharge `htilde` (change-of-scheme seed) → then `affine_serre_vanishing_general` unconditional →
feeds OpenImmersionPushforward Need#1. See [[02kg-seed-needs-change-of-base]].

The general-affine-open Čech-vanishing **seed** `HasVanishingHigherCech (affineCoverSystemGeneral R) F`. ## Your goal

Build project-local Mathlib infrastructure axiom-clean, one step at a time, going **as far as possible** in this iteration. Your output consists of definitions and lemmas that compile without `sorry` and whose `#print axioms` shows only `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`. You stop only when you hit a genuine mathematical blocker — not because one step is hard.

## Invariant

**No `sorry` in your output.** Every declaration you add must be fully proved. If you cannot close the current step: try alternatives first, then either prove a smaller sub-step or leave the declaration absent. Never add a declaration with `sorry`.

## Workflow

### 1. Orient

- Read `PROGRESS.md` for the objective: what infrastructure is needed, and why.
- Read the relevant blueprint chapter. It often contains the mathematical path to the ingredient you're building.
- Read `task_pending.md` for prior dead ends. Do not repeat them.

### 2. Scout the Mathlib API

Before writing any Lean, search for what already exists:

- `lean_local_search` for related names.
- `lean_leansearch` with a description of the mathematical content.
- `lean_loogle` for simple type patterns.
- Look for near-misses: a lemma that covers a sub-case, an instance you can compose, a `simp` set that contains what you need.

Even if you think you know the API — search anyway. Signatures change, instances disappear.

### 3. Build bottom-up, step by step

Start from the deepest missing piece identified in your scouting. For each step:

1. **Draft the type signature** and check it typechecks via `lean_diagnostic_messages` or `lean_run_code` before attempting the body.
2. **Write the proof.** Use Mathlib lemmas you verified exist. If a sub-step is missing, recurse: prove that sub-step first.
3. **Verify axiom-cleanliness** immediately after the step compiles: `#print axioms MyLemma`. Any `sorryAx` in the ancestry means the step is not axiom-clean — do not proceed until it is gone.
4. **Continue to the next step** in the chain.

Push as far as you can. Each axiom-clean step you add is permanent progress.

### 4. When stuck on a step — try before stopping

Before declaring a step impossible:

1. **Try a different proof route.** Reformulate the statement, weaken to a special case you can prove, use a detour through an equivalent form.
2. **Use the informal agent** if an API key is available (check `env | grep -E "DEEPSEEK|MOONSHOT|OPENROUTER|OPENAI|GEMINI"` first): `.claude/tools/archon-informal-agent.py` "Prove [goal] using only current Mathlib." Formalize whatever it suggests. If no key is set, skip to step 3.
3. **Search more broadly.** The lemma you need might exist under a different name or in a namespace you haven't checked.
4. **Prove a strictly smaller sub-step** that is axiom-clean and genuinely useful — something that shrinks the remaining gap.

Only after exhausting these alternatives do you stop. **Comments describing an approach you haven't tried are not exhausted alternatives — attempt them first.**

### 5. Stopping

**Before stopping, run this self-review:**

1. Did I attempt every approach I wrote in a comment, TODO, or log entry? If not, go back and attempt them.
2. Are there adjacent declarations in the chain I could attempt with the infrastructure I just built?
3. Did I call the informal agent (or document that no key was available) before declaring the step impossible?
4. Is there any approach I thought of but skipped? Attempt it — a failed attempt with a named error is better than a comment.

Stop only when all four answers are "yes" or "not applicable." **Writing a comment about a possible approach counts as identifying it — which means you should attempt it.**

**You are fully authorized to make every mathematical and structural decision within your assigned file without waiting for the planner.** "I'll let the planner decide which route to take" is never a valid stop reason — pick the most promising route, attempt it, document the outcome. The planner corrects route choices next iter; an idle session corrects nothing.

10	/-!
11	# Sub-brick A: identifying the evaluated augmented Čech section complex
12	  (blueprint `lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma` … `lem:cechSection_contractible`)
13	
14	This file is the shared "Sub-brick A" chain that
15	
16	1. identifies the degree-`p` Čech-nerve backbone `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj [p]` with
17	   the coproduct `∐_σ Over.mk j_σ` in `Over X` (`cechBackbone_left_sigma`);
18	2. decomposes the push-pull object `pushPullObj F Y_p` as a product in `X.Modules`
19	   (`pushPull_sigma_iso`) — the single new-infra leaf;
20	3. identifies the sections of each leg over an open `V` with `Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`
21	   (`pushPull_leg_sections`);
22	4. assembles the degreewise section isomorphism `Γ(V, pushPullObj F Y_p) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`
23	   (`pushPull_eval_prod_iso`);
24	5. promotes these degreewise isos to a complex isomorphism `D ≅ D'`
25	   (`cechSection_complex_iso`); and
26	6. produces the contracting homotopy `Homotopy (𝟙 D') 0` on the concrete complex whenever
27	   `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix` (`cechSection_contractible`).
28	
29	The result is consumed by `CechAugmentedResolution.lean` to close the residual `hSec`.
30	
31	Blueprint: §Sub-brick A decomposition, `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex`,
32	lemmas `lem:cech_backbone_left_sigma` through `lem:cechSection_contractible`.
33	-/
34	
35	universe u
36	
37	open CategoryTheory Limits Opposite
38	
39	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
40	
41	open Scheme.Modules
42	
43	variable {X : Scheme.{u}}
44	
45	/-! ## Stub 1 — Geometric backbone identification -/
46	
47	/- Planner strategy:
48	Goal: `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op [p]) ≅ ∐ fun σ => Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))`
49	in `Over X`.
50	
51	Route:
52	(a) UNPACK `coverCechNerveOver`: it is `Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom`,
53	    so the degree-`p` object is `Over.mk ((coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom.app (mk p))`.
54	    The underlying scheme is `(coverCechNerve 𝒰).left.obj (op (mk p))` — the `(p+1)`-fold
55	    fibre power of `coverArrow 𝒰 = Arrow.mk (Sigma.desc 𝒰.f)` over `X`.
56	
57	(b) DISTRIBUTE: coproducts distribute over finite fibre products in `Scheme`
58	    (`Sigma.fiberProduct_sigma` or similar Mathlib anchor):
59	    `(∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ×_X ⋯ ×_X (∐ᵢ Uᵢ) ≅ ∐_σ (U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p})`
60	    for `σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀`.
61	
62	(c) INTERSECT: each factor `U_{σ 0} ×_X ⋯ ×_X U_{σ p}` is the scheme-level intersection
63	    (fibre product of open immersions over `X`), which is the open subscheme
64	    `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ` with structure map `Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)`.
65	
66	(d) IDENTIFY: the structure map of the `σ`-component is the open immersion `j_σ`, and the
67	    universal map out of the coproduct is `Sigma.desc (fun σ => j_σ)`, making the LHS
68	    equal to `∐_σ Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))` as an `Over X` object.
69	
70	Key Mathlib anchors:
71	- `Scheme.pullback_openCover_iSup` or sigma-fibre-product distribution in `Scheme`
72	- `Scheme.IsOpenImmersion.isPullback` (open immersions are pullback-stable)
73	- `ColimitCocone` machinery for the coproduct in `Over X`
74	
75	Difficulty: MEDIUM — geometric bookkeeping, not sheaf theory. -/
76	noncomputable def cechBackbone_left_sigma (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀] (p : ℕ) :
77	    (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p)) ≅
78	    ∐ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
79	      Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)) :=
80	  sorry
81	
82	/-! ## Stub 2 — Push-pull over the Čech backbone is the product over intersection opens -/
83	
84	/- Planner strategy:
85	Goal: `pushPullObj F Y_p ≅ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)))` in `X.Modules`.
86	where `Y_p = (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (op (mk p))`.
87	
88	This is THE new-infra leaf. The key observation is that, although the opens `U_σ ⊆ X` OVERLAP
89	inside `X`, they are DISJOINT as components of the coproduct scheme `Y_p = ∐_σ U_σ`.
90	
91	Route:
92	(a) TRANSPORT via `cechBackbone_left_sigma`: by the iso from Stub 1, we may work with the
93	    coproduct `∐_σ Over.mk j_σ` instead of `Y_p`.
94	
95	(b) BUILD comparison map:
96	    `pushPullObj F Y_p ⟶ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)`
97	    from the projections `pushPullMap F (ι_σ) : pushPullObj F Y_p ⟶ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)`
98	    induced by the coproduct inclusions `ι_σ : Over.mk j_σ ⟶ Y_p` (going backwards via
99	    the pushPullFunctor, which is contravariant on `Over X`).
100	
101	(c) CHECK iso via `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf`:
102	    The forgetful functor `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf = SheafOfModules.forget ⋙
103	    PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf ...` is faithful, reflects isos, and preserves limits
104	    (`Sheaf.lean:75–78`). So it suffices to verify the comparison is an iso at the
105	    `Ab`-presheaf level.
106	
107	(d) PRESHEAF-LEVEL ISO: on `Ab`-presheaves, this is the indexed disjoint-union decomposition.
108	    Since the components of `∐_σ U_σ` are disjoint in the coproduct topology:
109	    * Iterate the binary `TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint` (Lean name: same) over the
110	      finite index set `{σ : Fin(p+1) → 𝒰.I₀}`.
111	    * Or use `Scheme.coprodPresheafObjIso` (sections over a binary coproduct scheme = product)
112	      as the binary building block and iterate.
113	    The result: for any open `W` in `Y_p`, `(q_p^* F).val.obj (op W) ≅ ∏_σ (j_σ^* F).val.obj (op (W_σ))`
114	    where `W_σ = (ι_σ)⁻¹W` is the trace on the σ-component.
115	
116	(e) TRANSPORT back through `toPresheaf` to get the iso in `X.Modules`.
117	
118	Key Mathlib anchors:
119	- `TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint` (Topology/Sheaves/SheafCondition/PairwiseIntersections.lean)
120	- `Scheme.coprodPresheafObjIso` (AlgebraicGeometry/Limits.lean)
121	- `SheafOfModules.forget` faithfulness and iso-reflection (`Sheaf.lean:75–78`)
122	
123	Difficulty: HARD (genuine new sheaf infra — the single new-infra leaf of the chain). -/
124	set_option synthInstance.maxHeartbeats 800000 in
125	noncomputable def pushPull_sigma_iso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
126	    (F : X.Modules) (p : ℕ) :
127	    pushPullObj F ((coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p))) ≅
128	    ∏ᶜ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>
129	      pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))) :=
130	  sorry

132	/-! ## Stub 3 — Per-leg section identification -/
133	
134	/- Planner strategy:
135	Goal: `Γ(V, pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)) ≅ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)` as `Ab` objects,
136	where `j_σ = Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ) : (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).toScheme ⟶ X`.
137	
138	Three off-the-shelf identifications, chained:
139	
140	(1) PUSHFORWARD SECTIONS = PREIMAGE SECTIONS (`pushforward_obj_obj`, `rfl`, Sheaf.lean:155):
141	    `Γ(V, (j_σ)_* N) = Γ(j_σ⁻¹V, N)` for any `N : (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).toScheme.Modules`.
142	    Apply to `N = (j_σ)^* F = Scheme.Modules.pullback j_σ |>.obj F`.
143	
144	(2) PULLBACK ALONG OPEN IMMERSION = RESTRICTION (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`, Sheaf.lean:371):
145	    `(j_σ)^* F ≅ F.restrict j_σ` as `(coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ).toScheme.Modules` objects.
146	    This is already used in `QcohRestrictBasicOpen.lean:113–114,248`.
147	
148	(3) SECTIONS OF RESTRICTION = SECTIONS OF IMAGE-PREIMAGE (`restrict_obj`, `rfl`, Sheaf.lean:328):
149	    `Γ(W, F.restrict j_σ) = Γ(j_σ ''ᵁ W, F)` for any `W` in the source scheme.
150	    Applied to `W = j_σ⁻¹V`: `j_σ ''ᵁ (j_σ⁻¹V) = U_σ ∩ V` (since `j_σ` is an open
151	    immersion: image-of-preimage = intersection with image = `U_σ ∩ V`).
152	
153	Compose (1)+(2)+(3): `Γ(V, (j_σ)_*(j_σ)^*F) = Γ(j_σ⁻¹V, (j_σ)^*F) ≅ Γ(j_σ⁻¹V, F.restrict j_σ)
154	= Γ(j_σ ''ᵁ (j_σ⁻¹V), F) = Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`.
155	
156	Key Lean names:
157	- `Scheme.Modules.pushforward_obj_obj` (rfl)
158	- `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback`
159	- `Scheme.Modules.restrict_obj` (rfl)
160	- `Opens.image_preimage` or `IsOpenImmersion.image_preimage_eq_inf` for the final equality
161	
162	Difficulty: LOW (three off-the-shelf steps, two of them rfl). -/
163	noncomputable def pushPull_leg_sections (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
164	    (F : X.Modules) {p : ℕ} (σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀)
165	    (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
166	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj
167	          (pushPullObj F (Over.mk (Scheme.Opens.ι (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ))))).presheaf.obj
168	        (Opposite.op V) ≅
169	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj F).presheaf.obj
170	        (Opposite.op (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V)) :=
171	  sorry

173	/-! ## Stub 4 — Degreewise section identification of the Čech backbone -/
174	
175	/- Planner strategy:
176	Goal: `Γ(V, pushPullObj F Y_p) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)` as `Ab` objects.
177	
178	Assemble three pieces in sequence:
179	
180	(A) PRODUCT DECOMPOSITION (`pushPull_sigma_iso`, Stub 2):
181	    `pushPullObj F Y_p ≅ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)`.
182	
183	(B) EVALUATION PRESERVES PRODUCTS (`SheafOfModules.evaluationPreservesLimitsOfShape`,
184	    `Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/Limits.lean:85`):

194	- `pushPull_sigma_iso` (Stub 2)
195	- `SheafOfModules.evaluationPreservesLimitsOfShape` (or `preservesLimitsOfShape_evaluation`)
196	- `pushPull_leg_sections` (Stub 3)

200	noncomputable def pushPull_eval_prod_iso (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) [Finite 𝒰.I₀]
201	    (F : X.Modules) (p : ℕ) (V : TopologicalSpace.Opens X) :
202	    ((SheafOfModules.forget X.ringCatSheaf).obj
203	          (pushPullObj F
204	            ((coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj (Opposite.op (SimplexCategory.mk p))))).presheaf.obj
205	        (Opposite.op V) ≅
206	    ∏ᶜ fun σ : Fin (p + 1) → 𝒰.I₀ =>

211	/-! ## Stub 5 — Complex-level iso: evaluated augmented Čech section complex ≅ concrete complex -/
212

214	Goal: `D ≅ D'` as `CochainComplex AddCommGrpCat ℕ`, where
215	  - `D = (GV.mapHomologicalComplex cc).obj Kp` is the evaluated augmented Čech section complex
216	    (GV = `PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf ⋙ evaluation(op V)`,

275	This is PURELY COMBINATORIAL — no affine vanishing, no qcoh, no tilde.
276	
277	Route:
278	
279	(A) IDENTIFY THE FAMILY: `U'_σ := coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V = ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V)`.
280	    `D'` is the alternating coface complex of the cosimplicial object
281	    `sectionCechCosimplicial (fun i => coverOpen 𝒰 i ⊓ V) Fp`.
282	
283	(B) MAXIMUM PROPERTY: Since `V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix`, we have
284	    `coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix ⊓ V = V`. Therefore `U'_{i_fix..σ} = U'_σ` for any `σ`
285	    (prepending `i_fix` does not shrink the open). Equivalently, the prepend map
286	    `σ ↦ Fin.cons i_fix σ` is the IDENTITY at the coefficient level:
287	    for each multi-index `σ : Fin m → 𝒰.I₀`:
288	      `⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (Fin.cons i_fix σ k) ⊓ V) = ⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V)`.
289	    This is because the `k=0` factor is `coverOpen 𝒰 i_fix ⊓ V = V`, which is ≥ every
290	    other factor (since `U'_j = coverOpen 𝒰 j ⊓ V ≤ V`); hence the iInf is unchanged.
291	
292	(C) INSTANTIATE THE DEPENDENT ENGINE: Set
293	    `A m σ := Fp.presheaf.obj (op (⨅ k, (coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ⊓ V)))`
294	    `δ m σ j := F.presheaf.map (homOfLE (le_iInf ...)).op`  (face restriction)
295	    `c m σ := 𝟙` (or the identity map via the equality from (B))
296	    Then the Dependent engine hypotheses hold:
297	    * `hu`: unit identity `c ∘ δ₀ = id` — follows from (B) (prepending `i_fix` at position 0
298	      recovers the same open, so the restriction is the identity).
299	    * `hsh`: shift identity `c ∘ δ_{k+1} = δ_k ∘ c` — follows from `cons_comp_succAbove_succ`.
300	    Call `CombinatorialCech.depHomotopy i_fix δ c` to get the homotopy maps, and
301	    `CombinatorialCech.depHomotopy_spec` to obtain `d∘h + h∘d = id`.
302	
303	(D) PACKAGE: Wrap the pointwise identity `depHomotopy_spec` as a `Homotopy (𝟙 D') 0` using
304	    `CochainComplex.homotopyOfEq` or by constructing the `Homotopy` directly from the maps.

307	- `CombinatorialCech.depDiff` (CechAcyclic.lean, namespace `CombinatorialCech`)
308	- `CombinatorialCech.depHomotopy`
309	- `CombinatorialCech.depHomotopy_spec`

321	    let D' := sectionCechComplex (fun i : 𝒰.I₀ => coverOpen 𝒰 i ⊓ V) Fp

1	# Analogy: Sub-brick A — identifying `Γ(V, pushPullObj F Y_p) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug
7	subbrickA
8	
9	## Iteration
10	055
11	
12	## Question
13	For the evaluated-at-`V` augmented Čech section complex (`V ≤ coverOpen 𝒰 i`), identify
14	degreewise and on differentials
15	`Γ(V, pushPullObj F Y_p) ≅ ∏_{σ : Fin(p+1)→I} Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`.
16	(Q1) Does Mathlib give "sections of a module sheaf over a coproduct scheme = product
17	of sections"? (Q2) Does Mathlib identify pullback-of-a-module-sheaf-along-an-open-
18	immersion with restriction, with definitional sections?
19	
20	## Project artifact(s)
21	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/CechHigherDirectImage.lean:135` — `pushPullObj F Y = (pushforward Y.hom).obj ((pullback Y.hom).obj F)`.
22	- `…/CechHigherDirectImage.lean:552,561,599` — `coverCechNerveOver` / `…Aug` / `CechNerve`; degree-`p` object is `∐_σ U_σ` with structure map `q_p = Sigma.desc (fun σ ↦ j_σ)`, `j_σ : U_σ = ⋂_k coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k) ↪ X`.
23	- `…/CechAcyclic.lean:1501,1513` — `sectionCechFaceRestr` / `sectionCech_objD_apply`: the existing product-of-presheaf-sections + alternating-restriction-sum machinery (`sectionCechProductEquiv`).
24	- `…/QcohRestrictBasicOpen.lean:113,248,283` — project ALREADY uses `restrictFunctor`/`restrictFunctorIsoPullback`/`restrict_obj`/`restrictFunctorComp`.
25	
26	## Decisions identified
27	
28	### Decision: Q2 — pullback along an open immersion = restriction (sections)
29	
30	- **Mathlib idiom**: For an open immersion `f : X ⟶ Y`, `Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctor f : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Modules/Sheaf.lean:319`) is the "better-defeq" model of pullback, with:
31	  - `restrict_obj` (`Sheaf.lean:328`): `Γ(M.restrict f, U) = Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` — **`rfl`** sections identification.
32	  - `restrict_map` (`Sheaf.lean:331`): the restriction maps are `rfl`-equal to `M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op`.
33	  - `restrictFunctorIsoPullback f : restrictFunctor f ≅ pullback f` (`Sheaf.lean:371`) — the bridge to the actual `Scheme.Modules.pullback` that `pushPullObj` uses.
34	  - `restrictFunctorComp` (`Sheaf.lean:392`), `restrictFunctorId`, `restrictFunctorCongr` for the bookkeeping.
35	  Why Mathlib chose it: `restrictFunctor` is literally `SheafOfModules.pushforward` along `f.opensFunctor`, so its object sections are `Γ(M, f ''ᵁ U)` by `rfl` — exactly the defeq the consumer wants — while `pullback` (a sheafification-of-a-left-adjoint) has no such defeq. Mathlib keeps both and ships the iso.
36	- **Project's current path**: `pushPullObj` is built on raw `Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom`. The project ALREADY adopts the `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` idiom in `QcohRestrictBasicOpen.lean:113–114,248,283–286` to convert `pullback`-along-an-open-immersion to `restrict`, with `restrict_obj` for sections.
37	- **Gap**: identical — Mathlib's idiom is the right one and the project already uses it elsewhere.
38	- **Verdict**: PROCEED (reuse the established project pattern: `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` + `restrict_obj`).
39	
40	### Decision: Q1 — sections of a module sheaf over a coproduct scheme = product
41	
42	- **Mathlib idiom**: only *binary* and only at the structure-sheaf / general-`TopCat.Sheaf` level:
43	  - `Scheme.coprodPresheafObjIso` (`Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Limits.lean:476`): `(X ⨿ Y).presheaf.obj (op U) ≅ X.presheaf.obj (op (inl⁻¹U)) ⨯ Y.presheaf.obj (op (inr⁻¹U))` — **structure sheaf, binary**.
44	  - `TopCat.Sheaf.isProductOfDisjoint` (`Mathlib/Topology/Sheaves/SheafCondition/PairwiseIntersections.lean:430`): for disjoint `U ⊓ V = ⊥`, `F(U ⊔ V) ≅ F(U) ⨯ F(V)` — **general `TopCat.Sheaf C X`, binary, same space**.
45	  - `Scheme.sigmaMk` (`Limits.lean:246`): `(Σ i, f i) ≃ₜ ∐ f` — indexed coproduct of scheme *spaces*, but **no section decomposition**.
46	  There is **no indexed (`σ`-family) version, and none for `SheafOfModules`/`X.Modules`**.
47	- **The off-the-shelf escape that shrinks the gap**: `SheafOfModules.evaluation R X` **preserves all limits** (`Mathlib/Algebra/Category/ModuleCat/Sheaf/Limits.lean:85,108`), and `X.Modules = SheafOfModules X.ringCatSheaf` `HasLimits` (`Sheaf.lean:50`). So if the degree-`p` object is recognised as a *product in `X.Modules`*, then `Γ(V, ∏_σ N_σ) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(V, N_σ)` is FREE. This turns "sections over a coproduct OPEN" (no API) into "sections of a product MODULE" (off-the-shelf).
48	- **The single irreducible new lemma**: package the coproduct as a product at the module level —
49	  `pushPullObj F Y_p ≅ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)` in `X.Modules`,
50	  equivalently `(q_p)_* (q_p)^* F ≅ ∏_σ (j_σ)_* (j_σ)^* F` for `q_p = Sigma.desc (fun σ ↦ j_σ)`.
51	  This is the disjoint-union/coproduct sheaf decomposition (a module on `∐_σ U_σ` is the product of its restrictions to the components, which ARE disjoint on the coproduct space even though the `U_σ ⊆ X` overlap).
52	- **Cost of divergence**: must be built. Port path: lift the binary `isProductOfDisjoint`/`coprodPresheafObjIso` argument to (i) finite-indexed `σ`, (ii) `SheafOfModules`. The lift to modules goes through `Scheme.Modules.toPresheaf` which is **faithful, reflects isos, and preserves limits** (`Sheaf.lean:75–78`) plus `M.presheaf.IsSheaf` (`Sheaf.lean:126`): build a module-linear comparison map, check it is an iso on the underlying `Ab`-presheaf via the sheaf condition over the disjoint component cover, transport back. NOTE the degree-`p` object identification `(coverCechNerveOver 𝒰).obj [p] = ∐_σ U_σ` (fibre powers of the open immersions = intersection opens) is its own geometric bookkeeping, separate from this sheaf-theoretic gap.
53	- **Verdict**: NEEDS_MATHLIB_GAP_FILL (one lemma; everything around it is off-the-shelf).
54	
55	### Decision: route the DIFFERENTIAL through the existing `sectionCechProductEquiv` machinery?
56	
57	- **Mathlib/project idiom**: `CechAcyclic.lean` already proves `sectionCech_objD_apply` (`:1513`): read through `sectionCechProductEquiv`, the cosimplicial coface differential IS the alternating sum `∑ᵢ (-1)ⁱ • (presheaf restriction `sectionCechFaceRestr`)`. That is the exact shape `SectionCechModule.dDiff` consumes.
58	- **Recommendation**: YES — express Sub-brick A's degreewise iso so it lands in the shape `∏_{σ} Γ(coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V, F)` and route the differential-match through `sectionCech_objD_apply` rather than rebuilding the alternating-sum bookkeeping. The only genuinely NEW work is the degreewise *object* iso (Q1 gap + Q2 off-the-shelf + evaluation-preserves-products); the differential is reuse.
59	- **Verdict**: DIVERGE? no — ALIGN with the existing project API (reuse `sectionCech_objD_apply`).
60	
61	## Recommendation
62	
63	Decompose Sub-brick A's degreewise iso `Γ(V, pushPullObj F Y_p) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)` into a
64	`\uses`-chain with exactly ONE new-infra leaf:
65	
66	1. `pushforward_obj_obj` (`Sheaf.lean:156`, `rfl`): `Γ(V, (q_p)_* N) = Γ(q_p⁻¹V, N)`. **off-the-shelf**
67	2. **[NEW]** `pushPullObj F Y_p ≅ ∏_σ pushPullObj F (Over.mk j_σ)` (coproduct→product at module level). Scope a prover lane: port binary `isProductOfDisjoint`/`coprodPresheafObjIso` to indexed + `SheafOfModules` via `toPresheaf` (faithful/reflects-iso/preserves-limits) + `IsSheaf`.
68	3. `evaluation` preserves products (`Sheaf/Limits.lean:85`): `Γ(V, ∏_σ N_σ) ≅ ∏_σ Γ(V, N_σ)`. **off-the-shelf**
69	4. `restrictFunctorIsoPullback` (`Sheaf.lean:371`): `(j_σ)^*F ≅ F.restrict j_σ`. **off-the-shelf, already used in `QcohRestrictBasicOpen.lean`**
70	5. `restrict_obj` (`Sheaf.lean:328`, `rfl`) + `opensRange`/`image_preimage`: `Γ(j_σ⁻¹V, F.restrict j_σ) = Γ(U_σ ∩ V, F)`. **off-the-shelf**
71	
72	Then chain to the existing `CechAcyclic.lean` machinery: land in `∏_σ Γ(coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ⊓ V, F)`
73	and reuse `sectionCech_objD_apply` for the differential. Four of five leaves are off-the-shelf
74	(two `rfl`), Q2 is already an adopted project pattern, and only leaf #2 needs a dedicated prover lane.

268:`simp`. Applying it to `pushPullMap` via `rw` rewrites the tail without forcing the
273:codomain `pushPullObj F Y₂`. Reusable pre-coherence brick for `pushPullMap_comp`. -/
294:for the composition law of `pushPullMap`. -/
319:hypothesis.** This is the body of `pushPullMap` with the underlying scheme map
324:kernel `whnf` wall). By construction `pushPullMap F g = rawPushPullMap g.left Y₁.hom
342:/-- `pushPullMap` is the `Over X`-instance of `rawPushPullMap`. Holds by `rfl`. -/
343:lemma pushPullMap_eq_raw (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) :
344:    pushPullMap F g = rawPushPullMap g.left Y₁.hom Y₂.hom (Over.w g) F :=
347:-- Composition law `pushPullMap_comp` is proved axiom-clean below (see `rawPushPullMap_comp`).
355:`η^a ≫ a_*(pullbackComp a p₁).hom`. Project-local helper for `pushPullMap_comp`. -/
372:helper for `pushPullMap_comp`. -/
388:`pushPullMap_comp` once the units and pushforwards are peeled. It is exactly
526:`pushPullMap_id` this assembles `pushPullObj`/`pushPullMap` into a genuine functor
528:lemma pushPullMap_comp (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ Y₃ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) (h : Y₃ ⟶ Y₂) :
529:    pushPullMap F (h ≫ g) = pushPullMap F g ≫ pushPullMap F h := by
530:  rw [pushPullMap_eq_raw, pushPullMap_eq_raw, pushPullMap_eq_raw]
534:Assembled from the object brick `pushPullObj`, the morphism brick `pushPullMap`, and
535:the two functor laws `pushPullMap_id` / `pushPullMap_comp`. Contravariant on `Over X`
538:`pushPullMap F φ.unop`. This is the functor the planner calls `G`; composing the
539:geometric Čech backbone `coverCechNerve` with it produces the cosimplicial
541:noncomputable def pushPullFunctor (F : X.Modules) : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules where
542:  obj Y := pushPullObj F Y.unop
543:  map φ := pushPullMap F φ.unop
544:  map_id Y := pushPullMap_id F Y.unop
545:  map_comp φ ψ := pushPullMap_comp F φ.unop ψ.unop
548:fibre power carries its structure map to `X` (the augmentation of `coverCechNerve`),
550:the constant functor. Project-local; obtained from `coverCechNerve` by
552:noncomputable def coverCechNerveOver (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) : SimplicialObject (Over X) :=
553:  Over.lift (coverCechNerve 𝒰).left (coverCechNerve 𝒰).hom
556:`coverCechNerveOver` augmented by the terminal object `Over.mk (𝟙 X)` of `Over X`. The
561:noncomputable def coverCechNerveOverAug (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) :
563:  SimplicialObject.augment (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰) (Over.mk (𝟙 X))
568:backbone `coverCechNerveOver` (read contravariantly, via `Functor.leftOp`) with the
569:push–pull functor `pushPullFunctor F = G`. This is the underlying cosimplicial object
573:  (coverCechNerveOver 𝒰 : SimplexCategoryᵒᵖ ⥤ Over X).rightOp ⋙ pushPullFunctor F
587:This is now *constructed*, not postulated: the geometric backbone `coverCechNerveOverAug`
591:`pushPullFunctor F = G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`, `(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. The whiskering
594:`(𝟙 X)_* (𝟙 X)^* F ≅ F`. The functor laws of `G` (`pushPullMap_id`, `pushPullMap_comp`)
601:  (CosimplicialObject.Augmented.whiskeringObj (Over X)ᵒᵖ X.Modules (pushPullFunctor F)).obj
602:    (coverCechNerveOverAug 𝒰).rightOp

120	`(j_V)_! O_X|_V` of the blueprint: on sections over `W` it is the free `O_X(W)`-module on
121	`Hom(W, V)`, i.e. `O_X(W)` if `W ⊆ V` and `0` otherwise.
122	
123	Project-local because Mathlib provides `PresheafOfModules.free` and `yoneda` separately but
124	not this composite, which is the basic building block of the free Čech complex. -/
125	noncomputable def freeYoneda : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules :=
126	  yoneda ⋙ PresheafOfModules.free X.ringCatSheaf.obj
127	
128	/-- The open underlying the `i`-th member of an open cover, as `(𝒰.f i).opensRange`. -/
129	def coverOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) (i : 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X := (𝒰.f i).opensRange
130	
131	/-- The intersection open `⨅ k, U (σ k)` indexed by a tuple `σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀`.
132	
133	For `κ = Fin (p+1)` this is the `(p+1)`-fold intersection `U_{σ(0)…σ(p)}` indexing the
134	degree-`p` term of the Čech complex. -/
135	def coverInterOpen (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ : Type} (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) : TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥X :=
136	  ⨅ k, coverOpen 𝒰 (σ k)
137	
138	/-- Reindexing along `α : κ' → κ` enlarges the intersection open: precomposing a tuple with
139	`α` keeps a subset of the indices, so the intersection grows. This `≤` supplies the
140	representable index-dropping maps that define the Čech differential. -/
141	lemma coverInterOpen_comp_le (𝒰 : X.OpenCover) {κ κ' : Type} (α : κ' → κ) (σ : κ → 𝒰.I₀) :
142	    coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α) :=
143	  le_iInf fun k => iInf_le _ (α k)
144	
145	/-- A coproduct injection transported along an equality of indices: `eqToHom` of the induced
146	object equality cancels the index change. A generic categorical helper used to discharge the
147	dependent-index bookkeeping in the simplicial identities of `cechFreeSimplicial`. -/
148	private lemma sigma_ι_eqToHom_transport {C : Type*} [Category C] {β : Type*} (B : β → C)
149	    [HasCoproduct B] {a b : β} (e : a = b) :
150	    eqToHom (congrArg B e) ≫ Limits.Sigma.ι B b = Limits.Sigma.ι B a := by
151	  subst e; simp
152	
153	/-- **The free Čech simplicial object of a finite open cover.**
154	
155	The simplicial object in `X.PresheafOfModules` whose degree-`p` term is the coproduct
156	`∐_{σ : Fin (p+1) → 𝒰.I₀} freeYoneda.obj (coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ)` and whose simplicial maps
157	reindex the multi-index `σ ↦ σ ∘ α` (along the order map of `α : [p] ⟶ [q]`) together with
158	the representable index-dropping inclusion `coverInterOpen 𝒰 σ ≤ coverInterOpen 𝒰 (σ ∘ α)`.
159

124	objects (`pushPullObj`, the planner's `Gobj`) and on morphisms (`pushPullMap`,
125	the planner's `Gmap`) — both axiom-clean and free of any functor law. The functor
126	laws `G(𝟙) = 𝟙` and `G(g ≫ h) = G(h) ≫ G(g)` are a *consumer* of the
127	pushforward/pullback composition coherence (`pushforwardComp` / `pullbackComp` and
128	their unitor/pentagon identities) and are deferred; see the note after
129	`pushPullMap`. -/
130	
131	/-- The object map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`,
132	`(Y, p) ↦ p_* p^* F`. Sends an `X`-scheme `Y` (with structure map `Y.hom : Y.left
133	⟶ X`) to the pushforward along `Y.hom` of the pullback of `F`. Project-local
134	object brick of the {\v C}ech push–pull functor (the planner's `Gobj`). -/
135	noncomputable def pushPullObj (F : X.Modules) (Y : Over X) : X.Modules :=
136	  (pushforward Y.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y.hom).obj F)
137	
138	/-- The morphism map of the push–pull functor `G : (Over X)ᵒᵖ ⥤ X.Modules`. For a
139	morphism `g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁` of `X`-schemes (so `g.left ≫ Y₁.hom = Y₂.hom`, the
140	over-triangle `Over.w g`), the contravariant functor produces the restriction
141	comparison `pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂`. It is the three-step composite:
142	the unit `η` of the adjunction `pullback g.left ⊣ pushforward g.left`, the
143	pushforward comparison `(pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, and the pushforward
144	of the pullback comparison `(pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom`, glued by two
145	`eqToHom` transports along the over-triangle `Over.w g`. No functor law is used:
146	this is a reusable pre-coherence brick (the planner's `Gmap`). -/
147	noncomputable def pushPullMap (F : X.Modules) {Y₁ Y₂ : Over X} (g : Y₂ ⟶ Y₁) :
148	    pushPullObj F Y₁ ⟶ pushPullObj F Y₂ :=
149	  (pushforward Y₁.hom).map
150	      ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g.left).unit.app
151	        ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
152	    (pushforwardComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app
153	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)) ≫
154	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward q).obj
155	      ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g.left).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback Y₁.hom).obj F)))
156	      (Over.w g)) ≫
157	    (pushforward Y₂.hom).map ((pullbackComp g.left Y₁.hom).hom.app F) ≫
158	    eqToHom (congrArg (fun q => (pushforward Y₂.hom).obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback q).obj F))
159	      (Over.w g))
160

163	X.Modules` requires

316	/-- Restriction of an `𝒪ₓ`-module along an open immersion.
317	This is isomorphic to the pullback functor (see `restrictFunctorIsoPullback`)
318	but has better defeqs. -/
319	def restrictFunctor : Y.Modules ⥤ X.Modules :=
320	  letI α : X.presheaf ⟶ f.opensFunctor.op ⋙ Y.presheaf := { app U := (f.appIso U.unop).inv }

332	    (M.restrict f).presheaf.map i.op = M.presheaf.map (f.opensFunctor.map i).op := rfl
333

335	def restrictFunctorAdjCounitIso : pushforward f ⋙ restrictFunctor f ≅ 𝟭 _ :=
336	  letI := CategoryTheory.Functor.isContinuous_comp.{u} f.opensFunctor (Opens.map f.base)
337	    (Opens.grothendieckTopology X) (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y) (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
338	  (SheafOfModules.pushforwardComp _ _) ≪≫ pushforwardNatIso _ (NatIso.ofComponents
339	      (fun U ↦ eqToIso (f.preimage_image_eq U).symm) fun _ ↦ rfl) ≪≫
340	    SheafOfModules.pushforwardCongr (by ext U x; exact
341	      congr($(f.appIso_inv_app_presheafMap U.unop) x)) ≪≫ SheafOfModules.pushforwardId _
342	
343	/-- Restriction is right adjoint to pushforward. -/
344	def restrictAdjunction : restrictFunctor f ⊣ pushforward f := by
345	  refine pushforwardPushforwardAdj (by exact f.isOpenEmbedding.isOpenMap.adjunction) _ _ ?_ ?_
346	  · ext U x; exact congr($((f.app_appIso_inv _).symm).hom x)
347	  · ext U x

349	      X.presheaf.map (eqToHom (f.preimage_image_eq U.unop).symm).op = 𝟙 _ := by

31	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

56	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules.
57	This is mostly useful to transport results from (pre)sheaves of modules to `𝒪ₓ`-modules and
58	usually shouldn't be used directly when working with actual `𝒪ₓ`-modules. -/
59	def toPresheafOfModules : X.Modules ⥤ X.PresheafOfModules := SheafOfModules.forget _
60	
61	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of modules is fully faithful. -/
62	def fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules : (Modules.toPresheafOfModules X).FullyFaithful :=
63	  SheafOfModules.fullyFaithfulForget _
64	
65	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Full := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.full
66	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).Faithful := fullyFaithfulToPresheafOfModules.faithful
67	instance : (toPresheafOfModules X).IsRightAdjoint :=
68	  (PresheafOfModules.sheafificationAdjunction (𝟙 X.ringCatSheaf.obj)).isRightAdjoint
69	
70	variable (X) in
71	/-- The forgetful functor from `𝒪ₓ`-modules to presheaves of abelian groups. -/
72	noncomputable def toPresheaf : X.Modules ⥤ TopCat.Presheaf Ab X :=
73	  toPresheafOfModules X ⋙ PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _
74	
75	instance : (toPresheaf X).Faithful := .comp _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
76	instance : PreservesLimits (toPresheaf X) := comp_preservesLimits _ (PresheafOfModules.toPresheaf _)
77	instance : (toPresheaf X).ReflectsIsomorphisms :=

86	/-- The underlying abelian presheaf of an `𝒪ₓ`-module. -/
87	noncomputable def presheaf (M : X.Modules) : TopCat.Presheaf Ab X := M.1.presheaf
88	
89	/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/

89	/-- Notation for sections of a presheaf of module. -/

96	    M.presheaf.map i.op (r • x) = X.presheaf.map i.op r • M.presheaf.map i.op x :=

104	def Hom.app (φ : M ⟶ N) (U : X.Opens) : Γ(M, U) ⟶ Γ(N, U) :=

115	@[simp] lemma Hom.sub_app (φ ψ : M ⟶ N) : (φ - ψ).app U = φ.app U - ψ.app U := rfl
116	@[simp] lemma Hom.zero_app : (0 : M ⟶ N).app U = 0 := rfl
117	@[simp] lemma Hom.id_app (M : X.Modules) : (𝟙 M :).app U = 𝟙 _ := rfl
118	@[simp] lemma Hom.comp_app (φ : M ⟶ N) (ψ : N ⟶ K) : (φ ≫ ψ).app U = φ.app U ≫ ψ.app U := rfl

content:
variable {X : Scheme.{u}} (U : X.Opens)

namespace Scheme.Opens

/-- Open subset of a scheme as a scheme. -/
@[coe]
def toScheme {X : Scheme.{u}} (U : X.Opens) : Scheme.{u} :=
  X.restrict U.isOpenEmbedding

instance : CoeOut X.Opens Scheme := ⟨toScheme⟩

/-- The restriction of a scheme to an open subset. -/
def ι : ↑U ⟶ X := X.ofRestrict _

@[simp]
lemma ι_apply (x : U) : U.ι x = x.val := rfl

instance : IsOpenImmersion U.ι := inferInstanceAs (IsOpenImmersion (X.ofRestrict _))

@[simps! over] instance : U.toScheme.CanonicallyOver X where
  hom := U.ι
=== NatIso.app ===

namespace CategoryTheory

open NatTrans

variable {C : Type u₁} [Category.{v₁} C] {D : Type u₂} [Category.{v₂} D] {E : Type u₃}
  [Category.{v₃} E] {E' : Type u₄} [Category.{v₄} E']

namespace Iso

/-- The application of a natural isomorphism to an object. We put this definition in a different
namespace, so that we can use `α.app` -/
@[simps (attr := grind =)]
def app {F G : C ⥤ D} (α : F ≅ G) (X : C) :
    F.obj X ≅ G.obj X where
  hom := α.hom.app X
  inv := α.inv.app X

set_option linter.existingAttributeWarning false in
attribute [to_dual existing app_inv] app_hom
